name: configure8 Internal Developer Portal API
what_it_does: Internal Developer Portal (IDP) with REST API — enabling platform engineering teams to catalog services and infrastructure, enforce engineering standards via scorecards, manage team ownership, and surface operational insights, with a focus on flexible ingestion from any infrastructure tool via customizable plugins and integrations.
use_cases: Cataloging services via configure8 API for service discovery and team ownership mapping
Enforcing engineering standards via configure8 scorecards for compliance measurement
Integrating tool data via configure8 API from GitHub, Datadog, PagerDuty for unified insights
Managing team ownership via configure8 API for incident routing and accountability
Automating catalog updates via configure8 API from CI/CD pipelines for continuous accuracy
not_for: Deployment automation (configure8 is catalog/standards — use Humanitec for deployment IDP)
Small teams without platform engineering function
Open-source self-hosted IDP (use Backstage for open-source)
best_when: A platform engineering team needs a flexible, opinionated IDP with strong scorecard capabilities and easy plugin extensibility, without Backstage's DIY complexity.
avoid_when: You need open-source self-hosted, deployment automation, or are a small team (<50 engineers).

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from configure8 account settings. REST API at api.configure8.io/v1/. Webhooks for catalog event notifications. GitHub/GitLab integration for repository-based service discovery. Datadog/PagerDuty/Snyk for operational data ingestion. No OAuth2 for third-party access.

pricing.notes: US company (New York). Founded 2021. Series A funded. Competing in IDP space with Cortex, OpsLevel, and Port. configure8 differentiator: flexible plugin system for ingesting data from any source; strong scorecard engine. Service catalog: components, resources, teams, domains. Scorecards: customizable checks with weights. Integrations: GitHub, GitLab, Jira, PagerDuty, Datadog, Snyk, AWS, GCP, Azure. Backstage alternative: no self-hosting required. Used by mid-to-large engineering organizations.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. Webhooks. GitHub/GitLab/Datadog/PagerDuty integrations. Scorecard engine. US company. SOC2. Annual contracts. No free tier.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Service catalog reads idempotent by ID. Scorecard reads idempotent. API mutations: use component ID for idempotent updates. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: IDP market is competitive and evolving — configure8's API may change as product matures
Plugin system is configure8's key differentiator — evaluate plugin extensibility for your tool stack
Scorecard configuration done in UI — API read access for scores but scorecard setup is UI-based
US data residency — verify for EU GDPR compliance requirements
Annual contracts — evaluate before committing; IDP space has significant vendor lock-in
No public API documentation as comprehensive as Cortex or OpsLevel — contact sales for API access

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token API key. SOC2 certified. Service catalog data (ownership, scorecard scores, infrastructure) is operationally sensitive. GitHub/GitLab OAuth integration credentials must be secured. US data residency. Webhook signatures recommended — verify implementation. No OAuth2 for fine-grained access control.
